description: Map /v1/chat/completions call chain; identify all files relevant to secondary development; produce .factory/library/ knowledge base.
implementation_steps: Read all key files listed in files_to_inspect
Trace call chain from HTTP entry to billing
Document findings in .factory/library/
verification_steps: All three .factory/library/ files exist and are non-empty
done_definition: .factory/library/ files created; call chain documented
risk_notes: Read-only; no risk

description: Add provider_type, risk_level, available_scope, visibility, manual_enable_required fields to model.Channel struct with GORM defaults.
implementation_steps: Add ProviderType string field with gorm default 'official_cloud'
Add RiskLevel string field with gorm default 'normal'
Add AvailableScope string field with gorm default 'public'
Add Visibility string field with gorm default 'public'
Add ManualEnableRequired bool field with gorm default false
Ensure all fields use pointer or have explicit defaults for backward compat
done_definition: Channel struct has all 5 new fields; go build passes
risk_notes: Adding nullable columns is safe; do not add NOT NULL without defaults

description: Ensure AutoMigrate adds new Channel columns on SQLite, MySQL, PostgreSQL; backfill existing rows with provider_type='official_cloud'.
implementation_steps: Verify Channel is in AutoMigrate list in model/main.go
Add post-migration UPDATE to set provider_type='official_cloud' where provider_type IS NULL or empty
Test against all three DB drivers
verification_steps: DB query confirms new columns exist
done_definition: AutoMigrate passes on all three DBs; backfill complete
risk_notes: UPDATE backfill must use WHERE to avoid touching rows already set

description: Create ProviderAccount model: id, name, provider_type, encrypted_key, created_at. Key encrypted via common/crypto.go AES.
implementation_steps: Create model/provider_account.go with ProviderAccount struct
Add BeforeSave hook to encrypt Key field using common.EncryptKey
Add AfterFind hook to decrypt Key field
Add ProviderAccount to AutoMigrate list
Add CRUD functions: CreateProviderAccount, GetProviderAccountById, DeleteProviderAccount
verification_steps: DB shows encrypted value, not plaintext key
done_definition: ProviderAccount table exists; key stored encrypted; CRUD works
risk_notes: Never log decrypted key; ensure CRYPTO_SECRET env var is set

description: Add nullable ProviderAccountId foreign key to Channel model; existing channels with NULL value continue to work.
implementation_steps: Add ProviderAccountId *int field to Channel struct
Do NOT add FK constraint (cross-DB compat); use application-level join
AutoMigrate adds nullable column
done_definition: channels table has provider_account_id nullable column; no regression
risk_notes: No FK constraint to avoid SQLite limitations

description: Regression: GET /v1/models returns 200 with model list after M1/M2 migrations.
preconditions: at least one enabled channel exists
implementation_steps: Write integration test or curl script for GET /v1/models
Assert HTTP 200 and non-empty data array
Assert 401 for missing token
done_definition: GET /v1/models returns 200; 401 for bad token
risk_notes: Requires running server or test harness with DB

description: Regression: POST /v1/chat/completions returns 200 with usage after M1/M2 migrations.
implementation_steps: Write integration test for POST /v1/chat/completions
Assert HTTP 200, choices array present, usage.prompt_tokens > 0
Run go test ./... and confirm no FAIL
done_definition: chat/completions returns 200 with usage; all existing tests pass
risk_notes: May need mock upstream or real API key for integration test

description: Add GetDefaultProviderType(channelType int) helper; wire into Channel creation to auto-set provider_type for known types.
implementation_steps: Add ChannelTypeDefaultProviderType map in constant/channel.go
Map official types (OpenAI=1, Azure=3, Anthropic=14, Gemini=24, etc.) to 'official_cloud'
Map aggregator types (OpenRouter=20, etc.) to 'aggregator'
Add BeforeCreate hook in Channel to set ProviderType from map if empty
verification_steps: DB backfill query confirms correct values
done_definition: Known channel types auto-get correct provider_type on create
risk_notes: BeforeCreate hook must not override explicitly set provider_type

description: Channel create/update API accepts and returns provider_type field.
implementation_steps: Ensure ChannelRequest DTO includes ProviderType field
Ensure GET /api/channel response includes provider_type
Add validation: provider_type must be one of the four allowed values
done_definition: Channel CRUD API accepts and returns provider_type
risk_notes: Validate provider_type enum to prevent invalid values

description: Channel selection (service/channel_select.go) excludes experimental_proxy from default pool; only includes it when explicitly requested.
implementation_steps: Add excludeExperimental bool param to channel selection query
Default: exclude channels where provider_type='experimental_proxy'
Only include experimental_proxy when caller explicitly opts in (internal user path)
verification_steps: experimental_proxy channel not returned in default selection
done_definition: experimental_proxy excluded from default channel pool
risk_notes: Must not break existing channel affinity / weight logic

description: Ensure pricing/quota calculation does not panic for channels with new provider_type values.
implementation_steps: Review GetQuotaByChannel for any channel-type-specific branches
Ensure no switch/case panics on unknown provider_type
Add unit test covering all four provider_type values
done_definition: Quota calculation works for all four provider_type values
risk_notes: Low risk — mostly read-only verification

description: Add ChannelTypeKiroGateway constant (value 58) to constant/channel.go with name and base URL.
implementation_steps: Add ChannelTypeKiroGateway = 58 before ChannelTypeDummy
Add 'KiroGateway' to ChannelTypeNames map
Add placeholder base URL to ChannelBaseURLs slice
Add to ChannelTypeDefaultProviderType map as 'experimental_proxy'
done_definition: ChannelTypeKiroGateway defined; name and base URL registered
risk_notes: Value 58 must not conflict; ChannelTypeDummy must remain last

description: Implement KiroGatewayAdaptor struct in relay/channel/kiro/ satisfying the Adaptor interface. Skeleton only — DoRequest proxies to configured base URL.
implementation_steps: Create relay/channel/kiro/adaptor.go
Embed or delegate to openai adaptor for OpenAI-compatible upstream
Implement GetChannelName() returning 'KiroGateway'
Implement GetModelList() returning configured models
Add compile-time interface check: var _ channel.Adaptor = (*KiroGatewayAdaptor)(nil)
done_definition: KiroGatewayAdaptor compiles and satisfies Adaptor interface
risk_notes: Skeleton only — actual upstream integration is out of scope for M6

description: Register KiroGatewayAdaptor in relay/relay_adaptor.go GetAdaptor factory switch.
implementation_steps: Add case constant.ChannelTypeKiroGateway in GetAdaptor switch
Return &kiro.KiroGatewayAdaptor{}
verification_steps: go test -run TestGetAdaptor returns non-nil for type 58
done_definition: GetAdaptor(58) returns non-nil; go build passes
risk_notes: Low risk — additive change only

description: Define isInternalUser helper: returns true when user.Group == 'internal'. Add 'internal' group to allowed groups list.
implementation_steps: Add const InternalGroup = 'internal' in service or constant package
Add IsInternalUser(user *model.UserBase) bool helper
Add unit test for IsInternalUser
done_definition: IsInternalUser returns correct result for internal and default groups
risk_notes: Group name 'internal' must be documented; admin must set it manually

description: In Distribute middleware, after channel selection, reject non-internal users attempting to use experimental_proxy channels with HTTP 403.
implementation_steps: After channel is selected, check channel.ProviderType == 'experimental_proxy'
If so, call IsInternalUser(user); if false, abort with 403 and error code 'experimental_proxy_forbidden'
If user is internal but channel.ManualEnableRequired && channel.Status != enabled, abort with 403
Add integration test
verification_steps: Normal user gets 403 on experimental_proxy model
Internal user with enabled channel gets 200
done_definition: Access control enforced in Distribute; tests pass
risk_notes: Must not affect non-experimental_proxy channels; error message must not leak channel details

description: Ensure channels created with provider_type='experimental_proxy' default to Status=disabled and ManualEnableRequired=true.
implementation_steps: Add BeforeCreate hook logic: if ProviderType == 'experimental_proxy', set Status=2 (disabled) and ManualEnableRequired=true
Add unit test verifying defaults
verification_steps: DB row has status=2 after create
done_definition: experimental_proxy channels created with status=disabled by default
risk_notes: BeforeCreate hook must only apply to experimental_proxy type

description: Ensure retry/fallback loop in controller/relay.go never selects experimental_proxy as a fallback when official_cloud channels fail.
implementation_steps: Review retry loop in controller/relay.go
Pass excludeExperimental=true flag to channel re-selection on retry
Add unit test: when official_cloud fails, retry does not return experimental_proxy channel
verification_steps: Integration: official_cloud failure returns 503, not experimental_proxy response
done_definition: Retry loop never falls back to experimental_proxy; returns 503 when all official channels fail
risk_notes: Must not break existing retry logic for non-experimental channels

description: Admin can disable experimental_proxy channel via PATCH /api/channel/:id status=2; cache is invalidated immediately.
implementation_steps: Verify existing channel status update API works for experimental_proxy channels
Ensure channel cache is invalidated on status change
Add test: after disable, subsequent requests return 503/no-channel
verification_steps: Next request to that channel's model returns 503
done_definition: One-click disable works; cache invalidated; requests blocked immediately
risk_notes: Cache TTL must be short or invalidated on write

description: Create Organization stub model: id, name, status, created_at. AutoMigrate on all three DBs.
implementation_steps: Create model/organization.go with Organization struct
Fields: Id int, Name string, Status int default 1, CreatedAt int64
Add to AutoMigrate list
Add stub CRUD: CreateOrganization, GetOrganizationById
verification_steps: DB table organizations exists after migrate
done_definition: organizations table created; no existing functionality broken
risk_notes: Stub only — no business logic; no FK constraints yet

description: Create Project stub model: id, org_id, name, status, created_at. AutoMigrate on all three DBs.
implementation_steps: Create model/project.go with Project struct
Fields: Id int, OrgId int (nullable), Name string, Status int default 1, CreatedAt int64
No FK constraint on OrgId (cross-DB compat)
Add to AutoMigrate list
verification_steps: DB table projects exists after migrate
done_definition: projects table created; no existing functionality broken
risk_notes: No FK constraint to maintain SQLite compatibility

description: Add nullable OrgId and ProjectId fields to Token model for future org/project scoping.
implementation_steps: Add OrgId *int and ProjectId *int to Token struct
Both nullable, no FK constraint
AutoMigrate adds columns
done_definition: tokens table has org_id and project_id nullable columns
risk_notes: Must not break existing token authentication flow

description: Verify and document that Token.Key is stored as hash (not plaintext). Add unit test asserting no plaintext sk- key in DB.
implementation_steps: Read token creation flow to confirm hashing
Add unit test: create token, query DB, assert stored key != raw key
Document hashing scheme in .factory/library/architecture.md
verification_steps: DB query shows no plaintext sk- value
done_definition: Unit test confirms key is hashed in DB
risk_notes: Read-only verification; if plaintext found, escalate immediately

description: Add provider_type varchar column to Log model; populate from channel info during RecordConsumeLog.
implementation_steps: Add ProviderType string field to Log struct with gorm index
In log creation path, set ProviderType from relayInfo.Channel.ProviderType
AutoMigrate adds column
verification_steps: DB logs table has provider_type column
done_definition: logs table has provider_type; populated on each consume log
risk_notes: Additive column; no existing log rows broken

description: Enforce privacy policy: when STORE_FULL_TEXT_ENABLED=false (default), do not write prompt or completion text to log.Other.
implementation_steps: Check STORE_FULL_TEXT_ENABLED env var (default false)
If false, omit prompt_text and completion_text keys from log.Other JSON
Always write prompt_tokens and completion_tokens counts
Add unit test for both settings
verification_steps: With flag=false: log.Other has no prompt/completion text
done_definition: Privacy policy enforced; token counts always present; text omitted by default
risk_notes: Default must be false; never change default to true without explicit user consent

description: After successful relay, verify user.UsedQuota and token.UsedQuota are incremented by log.Quota.
implementation_steps: Trace quota deduction path from BillingSession.Settle to model updates
Add unit test: mock relay, assert user.UsedQuota += quota and token.UsedQuota += quota
Fix any gaps found in the trace
done_definition: UsedQuota incremented correctly on both user and token after each request
risk_notes: Race condition risk — ensure atomic DB update

description: Integration test: POST /v1/chat/completions → DB log.quota > 0; BillingSession.Settle delta correct.
implementation_steps: Write integration test with mock upstream returning known token counts
Assert log.Quota matches expected calculation
Assert BillingSession.Settle called with correct actualQuota
done_definition: Billing session settle test passes with correct quota values
risk_notes: Use mock upstream to avoid real API calls in tests

description: PreConsumeQuota rejects requests when token.RemainQuota=0 and UnlimitedQuota=false; returns HTTP 402.
implementation_steps: Verify PreConsumeQuota checks RemainQuota before proceeding
Return typed error that maps to HTTP 402 in relay error handler
Add unit test with zero-quota token
done_definition: Zero-quota token blocked with 402; test passes
risk_notes: Must not block unlimited_quota=true tokens

description: Admin manual top-up API: POST /api/user/topup increments user.Quota and writes LogTypeManage log entry.
implementation_steps: Verify POST /api/user/topup endpoint exists and requires admin role
Ensure it calls model.IncreaseUserQuota atomically
Ensure it writes a LogTypeManage log entry with amount
Add unit test
done_definition: Admin top-up works; quota updated; log written
risk_notes: Requires admin role check; must be atomic DB operation

description: GET /api/channel supports ?provider_type= filter; requires admin role for experimental_proxy filter.
implementation_steps: Add provider_type query param to GetChannels model function
In controller, parse provider_type param and pass to model query
If provider_type=experimental_proxy, require admin role; return 403 otherwise
done_definition: Channel list filter by provider_type works; access control enforced
risk_notes: Non-admin must never see experimental_proxy channels

description: GET /api/channel (default, no filter) excludes experimental_proxy channels for non-admin users.
implementation_steps: In GetChannels handler, check caller role
If not admin, add WHERE provider_type != 'experimental_proxy' to query
Add unit test
verification_steps: Non-admin GET /api/channel returns no experimental_proxy rows
done_definition: experimental_proxy hidden from non-admin channel list
risk_notes: Must apply to all channel list endpoints, not just one

description: Admin API Key management: GET /api/token returns masked keys; DELETE /api/token/:id soft-deletes.
implementation_steps: Verify GET /api/token returns token list with MaskedKey (not full key)
Verify DELETE /api/token/:id uses soft delete (gorm.DeletedAt)
Add unit tests for both endpoints
verification_steps: GET /api/token response has no full sk- key values
DELETE /api/token/:id sets deleted_at; token no longer authenticates
done_definition: Token list returns masked keys; delete soft-deletes
risk_notes: Never return full key after creation; mask in all list responses

description: Admin usage_log query: GET /api/log returns entries without prompt/completion text; supports token_id filter.
implementation_steps: Verify GET /api/log strips prompt_text/completion_text from Other field before returning
Verify ?token_id= filter works
Add provider_type to response fields
verification_steps: GET /api/log response has no prompt_text field
done_definition: usage_log API returns privacy-safe data with correct filters
risk_notes: Admin-only endpoint; ensure role check present

description: Admin balance management: GET /api/user/:id returns quota/used_quota; top-up reflected immediately.
implementation_steps: Verify GET /api/user/:id returns quota and used_quota fields
Verify top-up via POST /api/user/topup is reflected in subsequent GET
Add integration test
verification_steps: After top-up, GET shows updated quota
done_definition: Balance visible and updated correctly via admin API
risk_notes: Admin-only; user must not be able to top-up themselves

description: Verify docker-compose.dev.yml starts the full stack; GET /api/status returns 200 within 30s.
implementation_steps: Review docker-compose.dev.yml for correctness
Ensure env vars for SQLite dev mode are set
Add health check to compose service
Test: docker compose up -d && sleep 30 && curl /api/status
done_definition: Docker compose starts successfully; health check passes
risk_notes: WSL2 path issues possible; test on Linux path

description: Write .factory/smoke_test.sh covering: models list, chat completion, balance check, experimental_proxy block.
implementation_steps: Write bash script using curl to test all 4 scenarios from user-testing.md
Script exits 0 on all pass, non-zero on any failure
Parameterize BASE_URL and test tokens via env vars
done_definition: Smoke test script passes all 4 scenarios
risk_notes: Requires running server and pre-seeded test data

description: Full regression: go test ./... exits 0; go vet ./... exits 0.
implementation_steps: Run go test ./... -count=1 -timeout 120s
Run go vet ./...
Fix any failures found
Ensure no test is skipped without documented reason
verification_steps: go test ./... exits 0 with no FAIL lines
done_definition: All tests pass; no vet warnings
risk_notes: Some tests may require DB or env vars; document requirements
